mappings.rationale: Directly covers the classification of animals from sponges through chordates, corresponding to the detailed animal taxonomy in this section.
Directly addresses viruses, the first major group in this section's classification of living things.
Directly covers monerans (bacteria and other prokaryotes), a major group in the classification outlined in this section.
Directly addresses fungi as a major kingdom in the classification of living things.
Covers the plant kingdom from bryophytes through angiosperms, directly corresponding to the plant classification in this section.
Covers insects, a major group within arthropods (uniramians), one of the largest animal groups in the classification.
Covers cnidarians (corals), a major animal phylum in the classification, along with their associated ecological communities.
Covers mammals as a major class within chordates, the final and most complex animal group in this classification.
Covers reptiles as a major class of chordates within the animal classification scheme.
Covers amphibians as a major class of chordates in the animal classification.
Covers an important extinct group of reptiles relevant to understanding chordate diversity and classification.
Covers the classification and diversity of microorganisms including bacteria, protists, and other prokaryotic and eukaryotic microbes.
Addresses the diversity and classification of microbial communities spanning bacteria, protists, and fungi.
Covers the diversity and classification of marine organisms across many phyla listed in this section including cnidarians, mollusks, echinoderms, and chordates.
Provides context for the classification of living things through the fossil record of extinct groups across multiple phyla.
